jobDescription: Iterable is the growth marketing platform that enables brands to create, execute and optimize campaigns to power world-class customer engagement across email, push, SMS, in-app and more with unparalleled data flexibility. We are an integrated, cross-channel solution— Iterable is built for marketers, trusted by engineers, and designed with intelligence. We know this space well: our product team built the growth systems that powered Twitter’s early success. We've recently raised a $50 M Series C from top-tier investors like Index Ventures and CRV, and hundreds of companies like Zillow, Seat Geek, and Box rely on us to captivate their many millions of users. Iterable's momentum grows daily and there has never been a more exciting time to join the team! We've been recognized as one of the Best Places to Work for two years running and earned ourselves a top 20 spot among the Saa S 1000. We've got a nation-wide presence with offices in San Francisco, New York, and Denver and and now we’ll be opening an office in London. We host an annual growth marketing conference where innovative marketers around the world come to share ideas. The Role: The People Operations team is motivated by the desire to empower and elevate all employees at Iterable so everyone can grow to their full potential. We thrive off of efficiency, excellent internal customer service, and removing roadblocks so our employees can be successful. As the HR Manager, you will share our responsibility in the United Kingdom to onboard, develop, and engage employees throughout their tenure at Iterable. Position Details: In this lead role, you will be the sole HR Representative in our London office (opening in September and planned growth to 10 employees by EOY). From answering day-to-day employee questions, over helping managers solve tricky employee issues, to running strategic initiatives in coordination with your US peers - to be successful in this role you need to be able to work independently and strategically but also get into the weeds. You will work in tandem with your peers in the US to cultivate Iterable’s culture and to roll out key people initiatives that will impact all of Iterable. Take ownership of onboarding and offboarding. Build processes in line with our values and partner with stakeholders to create a best-in-class experience that enables employees to thrive. Educate employees and managers on local employment laws, best practices, and company policies. Address and resolve employee questions, concerns, or grievances quickly, amicably, and with empathy. Coach UK managers to be world-class people managers by offering guidance on coaching, career development, performance feedback, etc. Recommend improvements to people programs, processes and policies to drive efficiencies and enhance the employee experience. Partner with your US peers on company-wide programs and own the UK roll-out and success. Ensure policy alignment across the global organization. Examples of programs include compensation planning, performance check-ins, engagement survey, etc. Ensure compliance by handling administrative tasks including entering data into HR information systems, auditing for accuracy or administering leaves (parental leave, vacation, sickness, etc). Develop and manage our Global Mobility program. Define and communicate global, scalable processes and policies. Partner and consult with outside immigration and tax advisor. Meet and greet candidates for interviews, coordinate video calls and candidate lunches, and provide a top notch candidate experience. Ensure everything we do aligns with Iterable’s culture and values to achieve outstanding employee engagement. The Ideal Candidate Will Be/ Have: Experience in a high-growth environment, preferably in Saa S or related software industry. Ideally you have experience working with a globally distributed team. You have significant experience in an HR role with exposure to HR Operations, Benefits, Global Mobility, Analytics, etc. You are a team player who doesn’t hesitate to go the extra mile or take on additional work to provide a white glove experience to the organization. You have an extremely high standard for organizational excellence and feel personally responsible for the successful end to end delivery of projects. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, understanding of cultural differences between countries. You have excellent project management and communication skills. You get things done by building relationships with your stakeholders and collaborating across teams. Your attention to detail helps you spot even the tiniest mistake. Ideally you have experience with ADP Workforce Now ( HRIS) and Greenhouse ( ATS). You will be required to flex your working hours so you can spend more time with your peers in the US as necessary. CIPD qualification. Be a champion of Iterable's core values--humility, trust, balance, and growth mindset--it’s part of everything you do! Iterable is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and strives to build a diverse and inclusive team. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.
